extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won a chaotic sprint after late crash split peloton under 1km, exposing himself without leadout train.
tactical_patterns: Kittel forced to sprint without leadout after team positioning failed, still won—suggests pure power trumps organization in chaotic finishes.
Sagan survived late crash but immediately contested sprint from disadvantaged position, revealing elite recovery capacity under 1km.
Gaviria optimally positioned on Sagan's wheel pre-chaos but couldn't convert, indicating sprint initiation timing matters more than positioning after disruptions.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Won despite total leadout failure and exposed position, suggests peak sprint power returning after winless drought
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Crashed inside final kilometer yet finished second, extraordinary bike handling and recovery speed under race pressure
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: Had textbook position on world champion's wheel but faded to seventh, questions sprint endurance after repositioning efforts
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: Completely disrupted by late crash positioning, dropped to 11th suggests poor chaos management compared to Sagan
rider_signals.SAGIV Guy: positive: Israeli champion from third-tier team contested both intermediate sprints aggressively against more experienced breakaway riders
